---
title: "User Manual"
subtitle: "BLISS-RS Julia Ebook"
author: "Troy Altus"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    self-contained: true
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
---

# Cover

| | |
|---|---|
| **Title** | A Response Surface Methodology for Bi-Level Integrated System Synthesis |
| **Subtitle** | BLISS-RS: Recreated and Extended in Julia |
| **Author** | Troy David Altus |
| **Version** | 1.0.0 |
| **Date** | 2025 |
| **Repository** | github.com/altustd/bliss-rs-julia-ebook |
| **Live ebook** | altustd.github.io/bliss-rs-julia-ebook |

---

# Quick Start (One Page)

## Step 1 — Clone the repository

```bash
git clone git@github.com:altustd/bliss-rs-julia-ebook.git
cd bliss-rs-julia-ebook
```

## Step 2 — Install pixi (if needed)

Download from [pixi.sh](https://pixi.sh) and follow installation instructions.
pixi manages both Julia and Quarto automatically.

## Step 3 — Install Julia dependencies

```bash
pixi run setup
```

This downloads Julia packages listed in `Project.toml`.

## Step 4 — Render

```bash
pixi run render          # HTML to docs/
pixi run render-pdf      # PDF to docs/
pixi run preview         # Live preview at http://localhost:4000
```

The rendered HTML ebook opens in your browser at `docs/index.html`.

---

# Purpose and Scope

This ebook is a scholarly recreation and extension of Troy Altus's 2002 Master's
thesis at George Washington University (NASA/CR-2002-211652). It documents the
**BLISS-RS** (Bi-Level Integrated System Synthesis — Response Surface) method for
multidisciplinary design optimization, implementing all algorithms from scratch in
Julia.

**Audience:** Engineers and researchers with backgrounds in aerospace design,
multidisciplinary optimization, or surrogate modeling. Familiarity with linear
algebra and basic optimization concepts is assumed.

**What is new vs. the 2002 report:**

- Complete Julia implementation of all BLISS-RS algorithms.
- Interactive figures rendered from live code.
- Chapter 9: modern extensions (Gaussian processes, Bayesian optimization, Julia
  parallelism).
- Chapter 10: new application of BLISS-RS to solid propellant rocket motor design.

---

# HTML Navigation

The rendered HTML ebook provides:

- **Left sidebar**: chapter and section navigation.
- **Floating TOC** (right): jump to any section on the current page.
- **Search**: full-text search across all chapters (search bar in the top bar).
- **Code folding**: click any code block header to expand/collapse source code.
- **Code copy**: copy button on each code block (hover to reveal).
- **Dark mode toggle**: sun/moon icon in the top navigation bar.

---

# PDF Download

A rendered PDF of the complete ebook is included in the `docs/` folder:
`docs/bliss-rs-julia-ebook.pdf`. It can also be regenerated locally:

```bash
pixi run render-pdf
```

The PDF requires a LaTeX distribution (TeX Live or MacTeX). If LaTeX is not
installed, only the HTML version will render successfully.

---

# Environment Setup

All dependencies are managed through `pixi`. No manual installation of Julia
packages is required beyond running `pixi run setup`.

| Tool | Version | Source |
|------|---------|--------|
| Julia | ≥ 1.10 | conda-forge (via pixi) |
| Quarto | ≥ 1.9 | conda-forge (via pixi) |
| Plots.jl | latest | Julia General registry |
| Optim.jl | latest | Julia General registry |
| DataFrames.jl | latest | Julia General registry |
| LaTeXStrings.jl | latest | Julia General registry |
| Distributions.jl | latest | Julia General registry |

---

# Rendering and Live Preview

```bash
# Full render (HTML + PDF if LaTeX available)
pixi run render

# HTML only
pixi run render

# PDF only
pixi run render-pdf

# Live preview with auto-reload on file changes
pixi run preview

# Clear execution cache (re-run all code from scratch)
pixi run clean && pixi run render
```

The first render takes 15–30 minutes (Julia code is executed from scratch).
Subsequent renders use Quarto's `_freeze` cache and complete in under 1 minute.

---

# Code Structure

```
bliss-rs-julia-ebook/
├── src/
│   ├── bliss_rs.jl         # Core BLISS-RS algorithms
│   ├── supersonic_jet.jl   # Supersonic business jet BBs
│   └── srm.jl              # Solid propellant rocket motor BBs
├── 01-introduction.qmd     # ... through 10-srm-application.qmd
├── Project.toml            # Julia package dependencies
├── pixi.toml               # Environment management
└── _quarto.yml             # Book configuration
```

---

# Interpreting the Outputs

| Output | What it represents |
|--------|-------------------|
| Optimization history plots | Value of each design variable or objective per BLISS-RS iteration |
| Coupling residual plots | Discrepancy |Y_RS − Y_BB| between RS predictions and actual BB evaluations |
| Response surface contour plots | Surrogate model over two variables (others held fixed) |
| Error analysis plots | Normalized prediction error as a function of sample size |

---

# Known Limitations

1. **Simplified black boxes**: The Julia BB implementations are surrogates of the
   original ELAPS/AWAVE/CFL3D codes. Results are qualitatively correct but should
   not be used for actual aircraft or rocket motor design without higher-fidelity
   analyses.

2. **PDF rendering**: Requires a LaTeX distribution. On Windows, install MiKTeX;
   on macOS, install MacTeX. Without LaTeX, use `pixi run render` (HTML only).

3. **Compute time**: The BLISS-RS optimization loops in Chapters 7 and 10 take
   5–15 minutes on first render. Caching eliminates this on subsequent renders.

4. **3D plots (Appendix A)**: The `scatter3d` function from Plots.jl with the GR
   backend may not render interactively. For 3D interactivity, run the notebook
   directly with `using PlotlyJS; plotlyjs()`.

---

# Citation

> Altus, T. D., "A Response Surface Methodology for Bi-Level Integrated System
> Synthesis (BLISS)," NASA/CR-2002-211652, NASA Langley Research Center, 2002.
>
> This ebook (2025): altustd.github.io/bliss-rs-julia-ebook
